programmes.show_description: Comedy and variety show with the bespectacled Barker and Corbett. Featuring the sketches Small Parts, Allotment, Nobody's Parrot, Yoiks Tally Ho!, and The Worm That Turned.
Mirthful fun with Ronnies Barker and Corbett. Among the classic sketches are 'Ham it Up', 'Last Mango in Paris', and 'League of Health and Beauty'. The Manhattan Transfer sing 'Candy'.
Classic sketches and skits with Ronnies Barker and Corbett. The chucklesome highlights include Walk in Love, Sid and Lil, George and Eddie, and Yes Man.
Featuring the Mastermind sketch, join the dearly missed duo for more first-class comedy. Allotment, Broadway Malady and the ongoing spoof serial The Worm That Turned also feature.
Comedy and variety show with the bespectacled Barker and Corbett. Featuring the sketches Small Parts, Allotment, Nobody's Parrot, Yoiks Tally Ho!, and The Worm That Turned.
Mirthful fun with Ronnies Barker and Corbett. Among the classic sketches are 'Ham it Up', 'Last Mango in Paris', and 'League of Health and Beauty'. The Manhattan Transfer sing 'Candy'.
The Doctor realizes that he and Jo are trapped inside a Miniscope, a device that contains miniaturized life forms for entertainment.
Shopping from Home.
Oman: Desert Canyon: Steve leads an expedition in the Jebel Akhdar mountain range of Oman. With temperatures soaring, dehydration and heat stroke are a constant threat.
Bhutan: White Water: Steve heads to the Himalayas to visit the last unexplored river in Bhutan. Legend has it that at some point, the river drops off the face of the planet.
Oman: Desert Fortress: Steve and an elite team of rock climbers head to the desert of southern Oman to attempt the first ascent of a towering escarpment.
Borneo: Dark Shadow: The team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo to caves holding secrets that could rewrite history.

Lucky Piece: Constant danger and drama in the WWII Belgian resistance drama. Lifeline is landed with a very special agent and a wounded Polish engineer from Peenemunde.
Trapped: Series two of the classic World War II drama set in occupied Belgium. Monique is shot on the French side of the border, and her papers prove to be false.
Not According to Plan: Classic World War II series set in occupied Belgium. Natalie's new love Francois and a group of evaders are passengers on a train that is due to be bombed.

Shopping from Home.
Clarkson tests out the Aston Martin DBS and the boys attempt to spruce up some cheap Porsches. The not-so-young ones are also joined by legendary singer Cliff Richard.
Buckle up for television's top motoring show. The latest cars are put to the test, while the boys are joined by The Who frontman - and Jeremy's hero - Roger Daltrey.
The guys compete in a race to the ski slopes - Jeremy by road in a Ferrari 612 Scaglietti, while James and Richard take to the air. With comedian and marathon man Eddie Izzard.

Featuring the Swear Box sketch. Vintage laughs from the bespectacled comedy greats. Other humorous highlights include: Minister of Poetry, The Bank and Doctor's Orders.
More witty wordplay and perfect parody with Barker, Corbett, and guest Diana Dors. Sketches include: Gummidges Greetings Cards, A Further Diet of Worms, and The Worm That Turned.
Featuring the Confusing Shopping List sketch. More chucklesome skits courtesy of the bespectacled Rons, including Minister of Intelligence, Welcome Home and Rochdale Ringers.
First-class comedy and variety with one of Britain's finest double acts, Ronnies Barker and Corbett. The sketches include Cafe Hold Up, Money, Allotment, and Clock Shop.
